Definition.text: Reduced is an additive negative modifier term that decreases a quantity within the additive stage.

Purpose.text: Model additive penalty values with explicit language distinct from multiplicative Less.

Core Rules.text: Reduced participates in the same additive pool as Increased and is resolved before More/Less.

Internal Rules.text: Reduced values are expressed as negative additive deltas and merged with other additive terms before multiplier application.

Calculation Order.text: 1) base value 2) aggregate Increased and Reduced into additive total 3) apply More/Less multipliers 4) cap/round.

Priority Rules.text: Mechanic-specific Reduced text overrides generic negative phrasing assumptions from comments or non-official summaries.

Preconditions.text: The target stat must allow additive scaling and have scope-compatible source semantics.

Postconditions.text: Negative additive effect is integrated into the stage that feeds multiplier buckets.

Limitations.text: Reduced is not interchangeable with Less in storage or application semantics.

Caps.text: No universal Reduced cap is defined in this pass; downstream systems may constrain final outcomes.

Formula.text: final = base * (1 + Σ Increased_i - Σ Reduced_j) * Π More_k * Π (1 - Less_l)

Conflicts.text: Confusing Reduced with Less is the primary modeling conflict and must be normalized separately.

Exceptions.text: Specific game mechanics may cap negative values for specific systems without altering the token identity.

Hidden Rules.text: Maintain explicit token identity even when numeric behavior overlaps in narrow cases.

Developer Notes.text: Never replace Reduced with arithmetic against base to emulate Less or vice-versa.

Historical Changes.text: No documented core redefinition of Reduced semantics in this run.

text.note: Reduced remains additive as a negative scaling layer in the captured model.

text: 20% Increased and 15% Reduced yields +5% additive in the same stage.
Two Reduced sources sum as one negative additive total before multipliers.
Reduced is the same as Less
Reduced should be multiplied, not added with Increased
